title: Certified Leakage Contracts: Compositional Quantitative Bounds for Speculative Side Channels in Cryptographic Binaries

overview.one_sentence: A compositional abstract-interpretation framework producing per-function quantitative cache-leakage bounds on x86-64 crypto binaries under speculative execution, via a novel reduced product domain with three-way reduction operator.
overview.core_construction: Reduced product abstract domain D_spec ⊗ D_cache ⊗ D_quant with reduction operator ρ producing leakage contracts (τ_f, B_f) that compose additively.
overview.crown_jewel: The reduction operator ρ propagating speculative infeasibility into cache taint pruning and then into capacity zeroing — genuinely new abstract-interpretation theory with no direct prior art.
overview.primary_use_case: CI-integrated regression detection: flagging when a compiler upgrade or code change increases any function's leakage bound.
overview.positioning: Software-side complement to LeaVe (CCS 2023 Distinguished Paper), which verifies hardware-side leakage contracts on RISC-V.

mathematical_contributions.name: Speculative Collecting Semantics with Quantitative Cache Residues
mathematical_contributions.description: Extends Guarnieri et al. (S&P 2020) speculative trace model with a quantitative observation function that maps transient execution traces to cache-state distinguishability counts rather than boolean observations. The trace structure is adapted; the quantitative observation function over speculative traces is genuinely new.
mathematical_contributions.what_is_new: Quantitative observation function obs_Q mapping speculative trace prefixes to cache-state equivalence class counts, enabling bit-level leakage measurement for transient instructions.

mathematical_contributions.name: γ-only Soundness for Speculative Reachability Domain
mathematical_contributions.description: Proof that D_spec soundly over-approximates the speculative collecting semantics via a concretization-only argument (no Galois connection required), enabling the tagged powerset construction without a best abstraction function.
mathematical_contributions.what_is_new: Application to speculative trace domains where the concrete domain lacks the lattice structure needed for Galois connections.

mathematical_contributions.name: Tainted Abstract Cache-State Domain D_cache
mathematical_contributions.description: Extends CacheAudit's must/may cache analysis with per-line secret-dependence taint annotations. Core cache model adapted from Ferdinand et al. and CacheAudit; taint layer is new but follows standard information-flow annotation patterns.
mathematical_contributions.what_is_new: Per-cache-line taint annotations tracking secret dependence through LRU eviction chains.

mathematical_contributions.name: Quantitative Channel-Capacity Domain D_quant with Taint-Restricted Counting
mathematical_contributions.description: Abstract counting over taint-restricted cache configurations producing per-cache-set leakage vectors in bits. Extends CacheAudit's counting abstraction (Köpf & Rybalchenko CSF 2010) by restricting counts to tainted configurations only, significantly tightening bounds when most cache activity is non-secret.
mathematical_contributions.what_is_new: Taint-restricted counting: only cache configurations reachable through secret-dependent memory accesses contribute to the leakage count. Per-cache-set leakage vectors enable fine-grained bounds.

mathematical_contributions.name: Reduction Operator ρ for D_spec ⊗ D_cache ⊗ D_quant
mathematical_contributions.description: Three-way reduction operator propagating constraints downward: ρ_{cache←spec} prunes cache taint on speculatively infeasible paths; ρ_{quant←cache} zeros capacity contributions for untainted cache sets. Applied after join at CFG merge points with inner fixpoint iteration. Monotonicity and termination proofs require domain-specific arguments with no direct template in prior reduced-product literature.
mathematical_contributions.what_is_new: The specific three-way reduction across speculation reachability, cache taint, and quantitative capacity domains. No prior reduced product combines these three concerns. The interaction between speculative window pruning and cache taint removal at merge points requires novel convergence arguments bounded by cache geometry.

key_properties.soundness: ρ(d) preserves the concretization: γ(ρ(d)) ⊇ γ_concrete(d) — ρ never introduces false safety
key_properties.monotonicity: d ⊑ d' ⟹ ρ(d) ⊑ ρ(d') — enables fixpoint computation
key_properties.termination: Inner fixpoint at merge points terminates in O(|sets| × |ways|) iterations due to bounded cache geometry
key_properties.precision: ρ strictly tightens bounds over the direct product D_spec × D_cache × D_quant on programs with mixed speculative/non-speculative paths

mathematical_contributions.name: Quantitative Widening ∇_quant with Bounded Precision Loss
mathematical_contributions.classification: DEEP NOVEL (deferred, not claimed for v1)
mathematical_contributions.description: Widening operator for the counting domain that guarantees convergence, never undercounts, and over-approximates by at most 2^k for k tainted cache sets. Not required for v1 which targets fixed-iteration crypto (full loop unrolling). Would independently merit publication at SAS/VMCAI.
mathematical_contributions.what_is_new: Precision-bounded widening specific to counting domains over cache configurations.

mathematical_contributions.name: Additive Composition Rule for Cache-State-Parameterized Leakage Contracts
mathematical_contributions.description: B_{f;g}(s) ≤ B_f(s) + B_g(τ_f(s)) — the leakage of sequential composition f;g is bounded by f's leakage plus g's leakage starting from f's output cache state. Instantiates Smith (2009) Theorem 4.3 for the specific setting of cache-state domains with taint-restricted counting under speculative semantics. The algebraic identity is known; making it sound in this domain with convergence guarantees is non-trivial engineering.
mathematical_contributions.what_is_new: Domain-specific instantiation: cache-state parameterization, taint-restricted counting, speculative-semantics compatibility, and characterization of the independence condition on standard crypto patterns.

mathematical_contributions.name: Independence Condition Characterization for Crypto Patterns
mathematical_contributions.description: Verification that the composition independence condition admits standard crypto patterns: AES with distinct subkeys (holds), AES with related subkeys (partial, correction term needed), ChaCha20 (trivially holds — zero leakage), Curve25519 (holds if τ sufficiently precise). The genuinely new content is the characterization on specific patterns, not a new theorem.
mathematical_contributions.what_is_new: Pattern-specific analysis showing which crypto compositions satisfy the independence condition and explicit correction terms when they don't.

spec_branch.description: At conditional branch: fork state into ARCHITECTURAL (correct direction) and TRANSIENT(0) (mispredicted direction). Both states explore their paths; TRANSIENT states increment depth at each instruction.
spec_branch.pseudocode: spec_branch(s, cond) → s_arch ∪ {(pp', TRANS(0)) | pp' = mispredicted_target(cond)}

spec_fence.description: lfence/cpuid: collapse all TRANSIENT states — remove them from the abstract state, modeling speculation barrier.

spec_tick.description: Advance speculation depth; prune states exceeding window W.
spec_tick.pseudocode: spec_tick(s) → {(pp, TRANS(d+1)) | (pp, TRANS(d)) ∈ s, d+1 ≤ W} ∪ {(pp, ARCH) ∈ s}

cache_access.description: Model LRU replacement: update must-hit/may-hit sets per Ferdinand et al. For secret-dependent address: set taint bit on accessed cache line. Propagate taint through evictions (evicted tainted line marks the set as having leaked).
cache_access.pseudocode: cache_access(s, addr, is_secret) → update_lru(s, set(addr), tag(addr)); if is_secret: s.taint[set(addr)][tag(addr)] = ⊤

cache_join.description: must = must₁ ∩ must₂; may = may₁ ∪ may₂; taint = taint₁ ∨ taint₂ (pointwise)
cache_join.complexity: O(S × W) where S=64 sets, W=8 ways

quant_observe.description: For each cache set with tainted lines: increment the interval upper bound by the number of distinguishable tainted configurations added by this access. Total leakage = Σᵢ log₂(hiᵢ).

quant_widen.description: For v1 (fixed-iteration crypto): not needed — loops are fully unrolled. Skeleton: widen intervals to next power of 2.

reduction_operator.description: The crown jewel algorithm. Three-step reduction with inner fixpoint at merge points.
pseudocode_steps: 1. reduce_cache_from_spec(d_spec, d_cache): For each tainted cache line l in d_cache, check if the memory access that introduced l's taint is reachable ONLY via speculative paths with depth > W or post-fence paths in d_spec. If so, clear l's taint to ⊥.
2. reduce_quant_from_cache(d_cache, d_quant): For each cache set i, if all lines in set i are untainted after step 1, set d_quant.hi_i = 1 (log₂(1) = 0 bits leakage).
3. reduce_full(d_spec, d_cache, d_quant): Apply steps 1-2 in sequence. At merge points: repeat until fixpoint (inner loop terminates in O(S×W) iterations).
reduction_operator.correctness_argument: Each iteration of ρ can only decrease taint counts and capacity upper bounds (monotone decreasing on finite lattice). Lattice has bounded height ≤ S × W = 512. Therefore inner fixpoint terminates.
reduction_operator.fallback: Single-pass ρ (apply steps 1-2 once, no iteration): monotone by construction, weaker but functional.

fixpoint_analysis.description: Worklist-based intraprocedural analysis using Bourdoncle's weak topological ordering (WTO).
steps: 3. Process worklist: for each node, compute join of predecessors, apply transfer function, apply ρ at merge points
4. At loop heads: unroll up to UNROLL_THRESHOLD (10 for AES, 20 for ChaCha20), then apply widening
fixpoint_analysis.complexity: O(|V| × h × S × W) where V = CFG nodes, h = domain height ≤ S × W = 512
fixpoint_analysis.crypto_aware_unrolling: AES: 10 rounds fully unrolled. ChaCha20: 20 rounds fully unrolled. Curve25519: single ladder step analyzed, contract extracted, composed 255 times via composition engine (never hold full unrolling in memory).
fixpoint_analysis.memory: O(|V| × S × W) per function ≈ O(|V| × 512) ≈ 500 bytes per CFG node

contract_extraction.description: Extract leakage contract (τ_f, B_f) from fixpoint result.
contract_extraction.tau_computation: τ_f(s) = abstract cache state at function exit given entry state s. Computed by running analysis with parameterized entry state.
contract_extraction.B_computation: B_f(s) = supremum over all program points p of accumulated leakage Σᵢ log₂(d_quant.hiᵢ) at p.
contract_extraction.serialization: JSON format with cache-state transformer as transition table and leakage bound as lookup over abstract cache-state representatives.

sequential.complexity: O(S) per composition — constant in function size

call_graph.method: Bottom-up traversal in reverse topological order of call graph
call_graph.recursion_handling: Bounded unrolling (depth k=3), then widen contract to ⊤
call_graph.complexity: O(|E| × S) where E = call-graph edges

regression_detection.method: Run full analysis on bin_old and bin_new independently. Compute per-function Δ_f(s) = B_f^new(s) − B_f^old(s). Alert if Δ_f > threshold (default: 0).
regression_detection.output_format: Structured JSON: {function, delta_bits, old_bound, new_bound, cache_config, severity}
regression_detection.ci_integration: Exit code 0 = no regression, 1 = regression detected. GitHub Action wrapper provided.

complexity_summary.per_instruction: O(S × W × C) where S=64 sets, W=8 ways, C=speculative context count
complexity_summary.per_function: O(n × h × S × W × C) where n=instructions, h=domain height
complexity_summary.per_library: O(Σ_f cost(f)) — dominated by largest functions
complexity_summary.composition: O(|E| × S) — linear in call-graph size
complexity_summary.memory_per_function: O(|V| × S × W × C) ≈ 500×C bytes per CFG node
complexity_summary.total_library_time_estimate: 30-90 minutes for BoringSSL crypto/ on laptop CPU
complexity_summary.note: All polynomial in cache geometry (bounded constants), not exponential in program size

implementation_strategy.language: Rust (performance-critical domains) + Python (CLI, CI integration, contract serialization)
implementation_strategy.substrate: BAP or angr as binary lifter — 4-6K LoC adapter for ~150 crypto instructions

module_breakdown.description: IR translation + abstract transfer functions for crypto instructions
D_spec: tagged powerset, speculative forking/merging, fence handling
D_cache: must/may LRU with taint annotations
Product operations, ρ operator (3-5K correctness-critical), fixpoint engine
CLI, regression mode, CI integration, JSON output
Unit, integration, precision canary, CVE regression, property-based

implementation_strategy.timeline: 16-22 months for one experienced PL/security researcher

research_questions.question: Does the analysis produce sound leakage bounds?
research_questions.metric: 0 false negatives on small-key exhaustive enumeration
research_questions.falsification: Any function where abstract bound < true leakage

research_questions.question: Are bounds precise enough to be useful?
Does ρ measurably improve precision?
research_questions.metric: Reduced product bound < direct product bound on ≥1 benchmark
research_questions.falsification: ρ adds zero precision on all benchmarks

research_questions.question: Does regression detection catch known CVEs?
Is analysis scalable to real libraries?
research_questions.falsification: Majority of functions exceed time budget

research_questions.question: Does speculation awareness add value?
research_questions.metric: Detects leakage that non-speculative analysis misses on Spectre gadgets
research_questions.falsification: Speculative analysis adds no information beyond non-speculative

benchmarks.name: AES T-table
benchmarks.purpose: Precision canary, ground truth via exhaustive enumeration

benchmarks.name: RSA-2048 CRT
benchmarks.ground_truth: Known to have timing leaks in some versions

baselines.name: CacheAudit
baselines.comparison: Match or exceed on AES T-table bounds

baselines.name: Spectector
baselines.comparison: Show quantitative bounds subsume boolean verdicts

baselines.name: Binsec/Rel
baselines.comparison: Show quantitative bounds subsume CT verdicts

baselines.name: cachegrind diff
baselines.comparison: Catch speculative leaks cachegrind misses + formal soundness

ablation_studies.name: ρ ablation
ablation_studies.method: Reduced product vs direct product (disable ρ)

ablation_studies.name: Taint ablation
ablation_studies.method: With vs without taint filtering in D_quant

risks.name: ρ precision failure
risks.mitigation: Precision canary validates counting; diamond-CFG test; single-pass fallback

risks.name: ρ soundness/termination
risks.mitigation: Prove on paper first; single-pass ρ is monotone by construction

risks.name: Independence condition fails
risks.mitigation: 4 crypto patterns tested; Rényi fallback; correction terms

risks.name: Speculative bounds vacuous
risks.mitigation: Reduced-B fallback (drop speculation, keep composition + quantitative)

risks.name: Lifter adapter gaps
risks.mitigation: Restrict instruction subset; accept gaps as known limitations

fallback_paths: Direct product without ρ: sound but less precise — degrades to 'CacheAudit + speculation + composition'
Reduced-B: drop speculation, publish D_cache ⊗ D_quant + composition as core contribution
Rényi entropy pivot: if min-entropy independence fails, 1-2 months additional, 2-5× looser bounds

phase_gates.name: Composition Theorem
phase_gates.deliverable: Paper proof + independence on 4 crypto patterns

phase_gates.name: Precision Canary
phase_gates.deliverable: D_cache ⊗ D_quant on AES T-table (~4K LoC)
